description: Evidence items for OT security audit — mapped to IEC 62443-3-3 SRs, domain requirements, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. For auditors conducting NACSA s23 assessments and for OT security teams preparing for audit.
auditorNote: For NACSA s23 audits: the auditor should be NACSA-licensed with OT security expertise (GICSP or equivalent). Evidence should demonstrate both policy (documents exist) and operational effectiveness (controls are working). Passive evidence (logs, monitoring outputs) is more reliable than documentation alone.

network-segmentation.description: Evidence that OT network zones are defined, enforced, and monitored.

evidenceItems.name: Network topology diagram with zones and conduits
whatGoodLooksLike: Current diagram dated within 6 months
All zones labelled with SL-T
All conduits with permitted traffic flows
IDMZ clearly shown breaking IT-OT path
commonGaps: Diagram outdated
IDMZ shown but not implemented
Unknown devices not represented
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Compare diagram to live network using passive monitoring platform asset discovery

evidenceItems.name: IDMZ firewall rulesets (both OT-side and IT-side)
whatGoodLooksLike: Deny-all with explicit permit
No direct IT-to-OT Layer 3 routes
All permits documented with business justification
Reviewed within 6 months
commonGaps: Overly permissive rules (any-any)
Stale rules for decommissioned services
No documented justification for permits
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review firewall configuration; test blocked traffic is actually blocked

evidenceItems.name: OT protocol-aware DPI configuration
whatGoodLooksLike: DPI rules for each OT protocol in use
Function code filtering applied
Policy violation log showing DPI is active
commonGaps: Standard port-based firewall only
DPI licensed but not configured
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review DPI rules; send test traffic with unexpected function codes and verify block

evidenceItems.name: Zone register (name, assets, SL-T, rationale)
whatGoodLooksLike: All zones listed with asset counts
SL-T per zone with documented rationale
Reviewed as part of most recent risk assessment
commonGaps: Zone model defined once, never updated
SL-T assigned without rationale
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Cross-reference zone register against topology diagram and monitoring platform asset list

remote-access.description: Evidence that all remote access to OT systems is controlled, authenticated, and monitored.

evidenceItems.name: Remote access gateway architecture and MFA configuration
whatGoodLooksLike: Dedicated OT remote access gateway in IDMZ
MFA enforced for all users
All remote access through gateway — no direct VPN to OT
Session recording enabled
commonGaps: Standard IT VPN grants direct OT network access
MFA deployed for IT but not OT-destined sessions
Vendor remote access via separate unmonitored path
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Test remote access path from external network. Verify routing through IDMZ gateway. Verify MFA is prompted.

evidenceItems.name: Session recording retention and review evidence
whatGoodLooksLike: Recordings retained minimum 90 days
Evidence of periodic session review
Recording review procedure documented
commonGaps: Sessions logged but never reviewed
Retention less than 30 days
Only remote access recorded, not on-site engineering sessions
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Request sample session recordings from last 30 days. Verify completeness. Check retention configuration.

evidenceItems.name: Vendor access approval and time-limited credential records
whatGoodLooksLike: Per-session approval workflow
Vendor credentials time-limited and revoked after session
Audit trail of all vendor access with duration and actions
commonGaps: Permanent vendor accounts active between maintenance visits
No approval workflow
No record of vendor actions during sessions
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review vendor access procedure. Verify last 5 vendor access events had approval records. Verify credentials revoked after session.

monitoring-logging.description: Evidence that OT security events are captured, retained, and actioned.

evidenceItems.name: OT monitoring platform coverage map and alert records
whatGoodLooksLike: Coverage map showing all OT segments with sensors
Active alert log for last 30 days
Evidence of alert review within defined SLA
NACSA-relevant alert categories configured
commonGaps: Monitoring deployed but alerts not reviewed
Coverage gaps at remote sites
IT SIEM without OT-specific detection rules
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review coverage map against topology. Request last 30 days alert records. Ask how high-severity alerts are triaged.

evidenceItems.name: Log retention configuration and integrity verification
whatGoodLooksLike: SIEM retention minimum 12 months online, 7 years archive (NACSA s26)
Log integrity verification mechanism (hash, write-once)
Capacity monitoring alerts before exhaustion
commonGaps: Retention only 30-90 days — insufficient for s26 7-year requirement
Logs in modifiable storage without integrity controls
No capacity alerting — logs silently overwritten
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review SIEM retention config. Review log storage architecture for write protection. Check capacity alerts.

evidenceItems.name: NTP synchronisation evidence
whatGoodLooksLike: All OT components synchronised to common NTP source
Time accuracy within 1 second
Authenticated NTP configured
commonGaps: OT systems on different NTP sources with drift
No NTP on older field devices
NTP unauthenticated
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Query NTP status on sample OT components. Compare timestamps. Confirm log correlation across systems is possible without significant drift.

configuration-management.description: Evidence that OT system configurations are baselined, hardened, and change-controlled.

evidenceItems.name: OT configuration baseline documents per system type
whatGoodLooksLike: Baseline hardening guide for each OT platform (Windows HMI, SCADA server, historian, EWS, PLC)
Current configuration compared to baseline with deviations documented
Baselines reviewed annually
commonGaps: No hardening guide — default configuration in use
Baselines defined but never applied or verified
IT hardening standards applied without OT-specific adjustments
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review hardening baseline. Select one system type and compare live configuration against baseline.

evidenceItems.name: Configuration monitoring deviation alerts and resolution records
whatGoodLooksLike: Configuration monitoring deployed
Deviation alerts generated for unexpected changes
Alert resolution records — each either matches a change record or was remediated
commonGaps: Configuration monitoring deployed but deviations not actioned
Monitoring only covers IT components, not field devices
Alert fatigue — volume too high to review
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review last 30 days deviation alerts. Verify each alert matches a change management record or was investigated.

supply-chain.description: Evidence that OT vendor and supply chain security risks are assessed and managed.

evidenceItems.name: Vendor security assessment questionnaires and approved vendor register
whatGoodLooksLike: Security questionnaire completed for all critical OT vendors within last 12 months
Approved vendor register maintained
Contractual security requirements in all OT vendor contracts (SBOM clause, vulnerability notification, audit rights)
Annual reassessment
commonGaps: Vendor assessments at procurement only — never reassessed
Security requirements missing from OT vendor contracts
Approved vendor list for IT only, not OT-specific vendors
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review vendor assessment records for top 5 OT vendors. Review one OT vendor contract for security clauses.

evidenceItems.name: Transient device management procedure and records
whatGoodLooksLike: Documented procedure for vendor laptops, USB drives entering OT environment
Scanning records for transient devices before OT network connection
USB port control technically enforced
Vendor acknowledgement of policy before access
commonGaps: Vendor laptops connected without scanning
USB drives accepted without scanning
USB blocking policy only, not technically enforced
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review transient device procedure. Verify last vendor onsite visit had a scanning record. Test USB port blocking on an OT workstation.

backup-recovery.description: Evidence that OT configurations can be backed up and restored within defined recovery objectives.

evidenceItems.name: OT backup configuration and schedule records
whatGoodLooksLike: Backup scope covers all OT components (PLC programs, DCS config, HMI files, SCADA DB, historian config, network device configs)
Automated backups verified running
Offline copy maintained (not only on network share)
Backup integrity checks configured
commonGaps: Backup covers SCADA servers but not PLC programs
Backup stored on network share only — vulnerable to ransomware
Automated backup configured but not verified as running
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review backup configuration. Verify PLC programs explicitly in scope. Check last successful backup timestamp. Verify offline copy exists.

evidenceItems.name: Restore test records with RTO demonstrated
whatGoodLooksLike: Restore test within last 12 months
Test covers at least one PLC/DCS component (not just file-level)
RTO verified — demonstrated restore time within defined recovery objective
Operations engineering involved in test
commonGaps: Backup exists but restore never tested
Test covers file restore only, not functional restoration of control system
RTO defined but never measured
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review restore test records. Verify test included PLC or DCS restoration to functional state. Compare test time to defined RTO.

physical-security.description: Evidence that physical access to OT assets is controlled and OT zones have appropriate physical security.

evidenceItems.name: Physical access control records for OT control rooms and panels
whatGoodLooksLike: Access control on all OT control rooms, server rooms, relay/MCC rooms
Access log retained minimum 90 days
Access list review within last 12 months — removing personnel no longer requiring access
Tailgating prevention in high-security OT areas
commonGaps: Combination codes not changed after personnel changes
Access log exists but not reviewed
OT server rooms co-located with non-OT assets without separation
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review access control configuration. Request access log for last 30 days. Verify access list includes only current OT personnel.

evidenceItems.name: Field site physical security assessment records
whatGoodLooksLike: Remote substations, pump stations, field cabinets have security assessment records
Tamper-evident seals on critical field equipment
CCTV coverage of field OT equipment where feasible
Periodic site inspection records
commonGaps: Remote sites assumed secure because remote
Field cabinets unlocked or key held at site
No physical tamper detection on critical remote OT equipment
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review field site security assessment. Inspect sample field cabinet — verify locked and sealed. Check whether broken tamper seals were investigated.

asset-inventory.description: Evidence that OT assets are inventoried, classified, and configuration-managed.

evidenceItems.name: OT asset register completeness evidence
whatGoodLooksLike: All zones represented
Required fields populated (make/model, firmware, IP/MAC, zone, criticality)
Updated within 30 days
Passive discovery cross-checked — no significant gaps
commonGaps: More than 10% unaccounted vs monitoring platform
Firmware versions missing for field devices
Remote site assets not included
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Compare asset register to OT monitoring platform list. Select 10 random assets and verify accuracy.

evidenceItems.name: OT change management records
whatGoodLooksLike: Change requests for all known changes
Post-change verification records
Emergency change records with retrospective review
No unreconciled gaps vs configuration monitoring
commonGaps: Undocumented changes discovered by configuration monitoring
Post-change verification skipped
No emergency change process
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Cross-reference change log with configuration monitoring deviation log. Unreconciled deviations are changes made outside the change management process.

safety-system-security.description: Evidence that Safety Instrumented Systems are physically isolated and their integrity is maintained.

evidenceItems.name: SIS network isolation verification record
whatGoodLooksLike: Physical cable trace confirming no network cables between SIS and DCS/IT
Signed off by safety engineer and OT security engineer
Dated within 12 months or after any SIS maintenance
commonGaps: Verification never performed — assumed isolated
Data diode bypassed during maintenance and not restored
Physical access panel connections not traced
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Physical inspection — trace every cable from SIS controller back-panel

evidenceItems.name: SIS program checksum register with pre/post maintenance comparisons
whatGoodLooksLike: Baseline checksums at commissioning or last verified state
Pre-maintenance hash matches baseline
Post-maintenance hash matches expectation for approved change
Two-person sign-off
commonGaps: No baseline established
Checksums checked by single person
Hash comparison not performed after maintenance
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review checksum register; select one controller and verify current hash against register

evidenceItems.name: SIS key switch configuration records
whatGoodLooksLike: All SIS controllers in Run mode during normal operation
Key held by named individual — not hanging in cabinet
Log of key switch mode changes
commonGaps: Key switch left in Program mode permanently
Key hanging in cabinet lock
No awareness that key switch is a security control
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Physical inspection of all SIS controller key switches; verify key holder identity

identity-access-management.description: Evidence that OT access is individually attributed, role-based, and privileged access is managed.

evidenceItems.name: OT user account register with role assignments
whatGoodLooksLike: All OT users listed with systems, roles, last review date
No shared accounts unless individually justified
Leavers removed — last access review within 12 months
commonGaps: Shared accounts in use
Leavers with active accounts
No review process
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Extract user list from each OT system. Compare to HR leavers list. Any former employee accounts are critical findings.

evidenceItems.name: PAM deployment evidence and session recordings
whatGoodLooksLike: PAM tool deployed with OT credential vault
Sample session recordings capture full session
Privileged account list matches PAM vault
commonGaps: PAM for IT only; OT still using direct credential access
Session recording retention too short
PAM vault does not include embedded device credentials
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review PAM vault for OT systems. Request sample session recording. Verify no engineer can access PLC without PAM.

evidenceItems.name: Annual OT access review records
whatGoodLooksLike: Review covers all OT systems
Signed off by OT manager
Action items executed (removals, role changes)
Completed within last 12 months
commonGaps: Review conducted but actions not executed
Review covers SCADA but not PLCs/historians/network devices
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review most recent access review. Sample 5-10 accounts removed during review and verify changes applied in target systems.

evidenceItems.name: Default credential elimination audit results
whatGoodLooksLike: Annual audit conducted
Zero known default credentials in environment
Devices where defaults cannot be changed documented with compensating controls
commonGaps: Audit never conducted
Only covers newly commissioned devices, not legacy
Known defaults with no remediation plan
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review last audit report. Test default credentials on sample OT devices in maintenance window. Any success is a critical finding.

patch-vulnerability-management.description: Evidence that OT vulnerabilities are tracked and patched within a defined programme.

evidenceItems.name: Vendor advisory subscriptions and tracking register
whatGoodLooksLike: Subscriptions to advisories for all major OT vendors
Advisory tracking register showing each advisory assessed
Assessment records dated within 30 days of advisory publication
commonGaps: No vendor advisory subscriptions
Advisories received but not systematically assessed
Assessment only for IT-managed OT components, not field devices
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Request last 3 months of advisories. Select 3 and verify they were assessed against the asset inventory.

evidenceItems.name: Patch deployment records with vendor qualification checks
whatGoodLooksLike: Patch records show: advisory reference, vendor qualification confirmation, change management approval, test environment validation, post-patch verification
Applied patches recorded against asset inventory
commonGaps: Patches applied without vendor qualification check
No test environment — patches applied directly to production
Records exist for HMI/SCADA servers but not for PLC firmware
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Request last quarterly patch deployment records. Verify vendor qualification confirmed for each patch. For PLCs, verify firmware version against vendor latest.

evidenceItems.name: Compensating controls register for unpatchable assets
whatGoodLooksLike: List of OT assets that cannot be patched with documented reason
Compensating controls for each (network isolation, stricter access controls, monitoring)
Risk acceptance sign-off from OT manager or CISO
commonGaps: No formal register
Compensating controls documented but not implemented
No risk acceptance sign-off
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review compensating controls register. Select 3 unpatched assets and verify documented compensating controls are implemented.

incident-detection-response.description: Evidence that the organisation can detect, classify, respond, and notify for OT security incidents.

evidenceItems.name: OT monitoring platform deployment and alert records
whatGoodLooksLike: Sensor coverage map showing all OT network segments covered
Alert log showing active detection
Evidence of alert review and triage within defined SLA
NACSA-relevant alert types configured
commonGaps: Monitoring deployed but alerts not reviewed
Coverage gaps in remote sites
No OT-specific detection rules — only generic IT rules
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review sensor coverage map; request sample alert review records for last 30 days

evidenceItems.name: NACSA notification procedure and tabletop exercise records
whatGoodLooksLike: Written procedure with incident classification matrix
NACSA contact details current
Tabletop exercise showing team classified incidents within 6-hour window
Notification decision authority defined
commonGaps: Procedure exists but never tested
NACSA contact details outdated
Team unaware of 6-hour notification requirement
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review classification matrix. Confirm NACSA contact details are current. Review last tabletop exercise records.

evidenceItems.name: OT Incident Response Plan with OT-specific runbooks
whatGoodLooksLike: OT IRP separate from IT IRP or as dedicated OT appendix
Runbooks for: ransomware, HMI compromise, PLC program modification, SIS anomaly
Operations engineering involvement documented
Annual review completed
commonGaps: IT IRP used as-is — no OT-specific procedures
Operations engineering not involved in OT IR
IRP never tested or updated
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review IRP. Confirm operations management has reviewed OT sections. Check last review date.
